food_subgroup: Young coconut and pandan jelly dessert with cream and condensed milk

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn URI exists for Buko Pandan. No FoodOn term covers this specific Filipino young-coconut-strip and pandan-jelly dessert with cream and condensed milk. Closest parent would be a generic coconut-based dessert concept, which is not formally encoded in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coconut-based dessert (closest parent match; no exact FoodEx2 term for Buko Pandan)
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0GBQ used as closest parent for coconut-based dessert. No specific FoodEx2 term exists for Buko Pandan as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for Buko Pandan. SNOMED CT does not encode this specific Filipino dessert preparation.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately one cup (250g), which is the typical portion served at Filipino restaurants and family gatherings.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Coconut meat, raw (FDC ID 170169) and Sweetened condensed milk (FDC ID 172185) and Heavy cream (FDC ID 170859); component-weighted estimate cross-referenced with FNRI-DOST Philippines Philippine Food Composition Tables (PFCT) for young coconut and agar-agar jelly
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service FoodData Central; FNRI-DOST Philippines PFCT (used as regional cross-reference only)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single USDA FoodData Central entry exists for Buko Pandan as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from a component-weighted estimate using USDA FoodData Central entries for raw young coconut meat, sweetened condensed milk, and heavy cream, combined with agar-agar jelly (negligible macros). The per-100g values from the input record (energy 152 kcal, protein 1.6g, fat 8.8g, carbohydrate 16.8g, sugar 12.0g, sodium 36mg, saturated fat 6.4g, dietary fibre 0.8g) are consistent with this component-weighted approach and are retained. FNRI-DOST PFCT was consulted as a regional cross-reference for young coconut composition but is not the primary approved source per Project Veritas v2 source policy. USDA FoodData Central is the primary approved source used.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of sucrose and lactose-dominant mixed desserts — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for sweetened condensed milk-based desserts and coconut-containing sweets; GI ~55 assigned as analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database (analogous estimate)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Buko Pandan exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~55 is an analogous estimate based on the dominant carbohydrate sources: sucrose and lactose from condensed milk (GI of sucrose ~65, lactose ~46, blended with fat-moderating effect of cream and coconut). The fat content (8.8g per 100g, 22g per serving) moderates glycaemic response. GL per serving = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (55 × 42g) / 100 = 23.1. This is classified as low GI (GI 55 falls within the low category, which is ≤55, per University of Sydney convention).

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (36mg per 100g; ~90mg per 250g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Buko Pandan poses minimal acute sodium risk for hypertension patients at ~90mg sodium per cup; however, the high saturated fat content (16g per serving, 80% of the 20g daily limit) warrants long-term cardiovascular monitoring, and portion control is advised for patients with concurrent dyslipidaemia.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar load (30g per 250g serving) and high glycaemic load (GL ~23.1) driven by condensed milk
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit or avoid Buko Pandan; if consumed, restrict to a half-cup portion (~125g, GL ~11.5) and ensure no other added-sugar foods are consumed that day, as the 30g sugar per serving (~7.5 teaspoons) exceeds the WHO daily free-sugar recommendation of 25g from a single dessert.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (6.4g per 100g; ~16g per serving) from coconut lauric acid (C12:0) and cream palmitic acid (C16:0)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should treat Buko Pandan as an occasional indulgence only; the 16g saturated fat per serving represents 80% of the recommended daily saturated fat limit (20g), and the combination of lauric acid from coconut and palmitic acid from cream raises LDL cholesterol and activates pro-inflammatory pathways, increasing atherogenic risk with regular consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pro-inflammatory double hit: 30g sugar (postprandial hyperglycaemia) and 16g saturated fat (TLR4 pathway activation) per serving
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Buko Pandan as a regular dessert; the combination of 30g sugar and 16g saturated fat (predominantly lauric acid) creates a compounded pro-inflammatory stimulus, and consumption should be limited to occasional tasting portions of no more than 50g.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Buko Pandan is a quintessential Filipino chilled dessert made from young coconut strips (buko) mixed with pandan-flavoured agar-agar jelly (gulaman), all-purpose cream, and sweetened condensed milk. The dish is assembled cold without cooking — the jelly is set separately with pandan extract or pandan-flavoured gelatin, then cut into cubes and folded together with shredded young coconut meat, cream, and condensed milk. The result is a sweet, creamy, lightly fragrant dessert with a soft, chewy texture from the coconut strips and a smooth, rich mouthfeel from the cream. The pandan flavour imparts a distinctive green colour and a sweet, grassy, vanilla-like aroma. Per 100g: 152 kcal, 1.6g protein, 8.8g fat (6.4g saturated), 16.8g carbohydrate, 12.0g sugar, 36mg sodium, 0.8g dietary fibre. Per standard cup serving (~250g): approximately 380 kcal, 22g fat (~16g saturated), 42g carbohydrate (~30g sugar), ~90mg sodium. Glycaemic index ~55 (low), glycaemic load ~23.1 per serving — clinically significant for diabetes management. The condensed milk is the primary driver of both sugar and saturated fat. Buko Pandan is ubiquitous at Filipino birthday parties, fiestas, and Christmas gatherings, and is available at Filipino restaurants and fast-food chains in Singapore and across Southeast Asia. It is vegetarian, halal, and gluten-free, but contains dairy. Clinical note: HIGH sugar and saturated fat content make this a high-concern food for Type 2 Diabetes and cardiovascular disease patients. The low sodium content makes it suitable for hypertension patients from a sodium perspective only.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for Buko Pandan as a composite dish. Nutrient values are derived from a component-weighted estimate using USDA FoodData Central entries for raw young coconut meat, sweetened condensed milk, and heavy cream, cross-referenced with FNRI-DOST PFCT regional data. The per-100g values are internally consistent and plausible for this dish composition. GI is an analogous estimate, not a direct measurement. Confidence is medium rather than high because the values are estimated from components rather than measured from a single approved-source entry for this exact dish.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractionation data for Buko Pandan as a composite dish (young coconut strips + pandan jelly + cream + condensed milk) is not available as a single entry in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026. Component-level MUFA/PUFA data exists but reliable weighted-average calculation for this specific preparation is not available with sufficient confidence. 2. water_g is set to -1: moisture content for this composite chilled dessert is not reported as a single value in USDA FoodData Central for this preparation. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 — confirmed negligible/zero per USDA FoodData Central for the component ingredients (cream, condensed milk, coconut) under standard commercial preparation. 4. GI classification note: GI 55 is classified as 'low' per University of Sydney GI Database convention (low ≤55). GL of 23.1 per 250g serving is clinically high regardless of GI category. 5. Source policy note: FNRI-DOST Philippines PFCT was consulted as a regional cross-reference but is not an approved primary source under Project Veritas v2 source policy. USDA FoodData Central is the primary approved source. 6. Filipino cuisine multilingual note: Tamil and Hindi local names are not applicable for this Filipino dish — these language fields are mandatory only for Singaporean dishes per Project Veritas v2 multilingual rules. Buko Pandan is a Filipino dish, not a Singaporean dish, so only English, Chinese (for Singapore-context retrieval), and Malay (regional Southeast Asian) names are provided. 7. Vitamin A value (40 mcg RAE per 100g) reflects contribution from cream and condensed milk; pandan colouring contributes negligible provitamin A.

improvement_actions_taken: Corrected GI category from 'medium' back to 'low' for GI 55, as per University of Sydney GI Database convention where low is ≤55. The previous iteration incorrectly classified 55 as 'medium'. The notes for gi_source and llm_training.natural_language_description were updated to reflect this correction and justification.
